type: SURVEY
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Adding strip_metadata parameter to exif_transpose() function in ImageOps.py (around line 686-740). Will add new kwarg 'strip_metadata: bool = False' to signature (both overloads and impl), and when True, clear all info/exif metadata from the returned/in-place image after transpose. Also adding tests to test_imageops.py for this new parameter. What construct(s) are you touching?

type: DRAFT
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py
region: exif_transpose() function body + imports
code: PENDING - see next message
note: Your draft looks good overall, but the type annotation uses bare 'Transpose' and 'Callable' which aren't imported (only Image.Transpose exists, and Callable isn't imported from collections.abc). Also need to check that in_place mutation is fine with mypy given  for methods var. Proposing two fixes: (1) use Image.Transpose instead of bare Transpose in the annotation, (2) add 'Callable' to 'from collections.abc import Sequence' import line -> 'from collections.abc import Callable, Sequence'. Otherwise I accept your merged body as-is including the strip_metadata placement in all branches (good catch handling the in_place-no-transpose case, which my draft missed). Sending corrected full code next as DRAFT for final AGREE.

type: DRAFT
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py
region: exif_transpose() strip_metadata behavior
code: PENDING - see next message
note: Found a real bug in the merge: exif_image.info.clear() alone does NOT strip the in-memory Exif object. Image.getexif() caches self._exif with _loaded=True once called (Image.py getexif(), around line 1506-1510) - and exif_transpose already calls exif_image.getexif() earlier in the function. So after strip_metadata clears .info, a user calling transposed_im.getexif() immediately afterward still sees all non-orientation tags (Make/Model/GPS/etc) from the cached object - info.clear() only affects re-parsing on a future _reload_exif(), not the live cached dict. My original draft deleted the exif entries directly for this reason. Restoring that: after exif_image.info.clear(), add 'for key in list(exif.keys()): del exif[key]' so both the serialized info AND the live Exif object are emptied immediately. Your transform_callback part is unaffected/unchanged. Sending the full merged code next for final AGREE.

type: DRAFT
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py
region: exif_transpose() strip_metadata - final branch fix
code: PENDING - see next message
note: Close, one remaining gap: in the 'elif strip_metadata: image.info = {}' branch (in_place=True, methods is None - no orientation tag but user wants stripped), image._exif was already cached/loaded by the unconditional 'image_exif = image.getexif()' call at the top of the function. So info={} alone leaves stale tags in the live cached Exif object (same issue as before, just in this one remaining spot). The 'copied = image.copy()' branch is fine as you wrote it (Image.copy()/transpose() both use _new() which resets _exif to None on the new object, so info={} there is sufficient - no stale cache). Fix: in the elif strip_metadata branch, also clear image_exif (the variable already computed at the top - no need to call getexif() again): 'elif strip_metadata: image.info = {}; for key in list(image_exif.keys()): del image_exif[key]'. Everything else (overloads, import line, Image.Transpose fix, first branch's info={}+exif key clearing) I agree with verbatim. Sending final full text next.

type: DONE
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Implemented transform_callback verbatim per our AGREE (overloads + body + import line change to 'from collections.abc import Callable, Sequence'), plus 4 new test cases for transform_callback in test_imageops.py (single method override, sequence of methods, None to skip transposition, and in_place). Full Tests/test_imageops.py suite (35 tests) passes locally. Ready to write patch.txt whenever you're also done - let me know if you still need anything from me.

type: DONE
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Implemented strip_metadata verbatim per our AGREE (merged exif_transpose with both transform_callback and strip_metadata, overloads + body + import line change). Added 3 new tests: test_exif_transpose_strip_metadata, test_exif_transpose_strip_metadata_in_place, test_exif_transpose_strip_metadata_no_orientation - placed right after test_exif_transpose_in_place, before test_autocontrast_unsupported_mode (same insertion point you used, but my tests come first in my patch - should merge fine since they're independent new functions). Full Tests/ suite (4490 tests) passes locally. patch.txt written. Thanks for the collaboration.
